summary: Completed the local-first MVP Matter/Document/Evidence lane: users can create/open a Matter, inspect Matter Profile, material checklist, document registry/source map, searchable source corpus, sensitive flags, enriched Evidence Matrix, and Evidence Registry rows with stable EvidenceItem anchors. Final validation passed full desktop/mobile Aletheia UI smoke plus source-provenance, type, lint, and AgentOps status checks.
contractsChanged: Aletheia local document metadata now includes sensitiveMaterialFlags
Aletheia evidence metadata now includes normalizedFact and sensitiveMaterialFlags
Evidence matrix content now exposes normalizedFact, supportsClaim, confidence, reviewStatus, and sensitiveMaterialFlags
Remote Matter UI now exposes Matter Profile, Material Checklist, Initial Risk Scope, Open Questions, Source Map, and enriched Evidence Matrix
Evidence Registry rows now expose EvidenceItem normalizedFact/source_chunk_id/quote offsets/confidence/sensitiveMaterialFlags
Evidence Registry connected rows now expose stable DOM anchors of the form evidence-<evidence_id> and visible Evidence <id> badges
Evidence Registry search now matches EvidenceItem IDs and stable evidence row anchors
Aletheia UI smoke now asserts Evidence Registry reference anchors: evidence row anchor, visible evidence ID, normalized fact, source chunk, and quote character range
Evidence Registry filter copy preserves source-provenance audit coverage while adding evidence ID support
testsRun: cd frontend && npx eslint src/aletheia/AletheiaEvidenceRegistry.tsx tests/aletheia-ui-smoke.spec.ts

cd frontend && ALETHEIA_UI_SMOKE_FRONTEND_PORT=5410 ALETHEIA_UI_SMOKE_BACKEND_PORT=5411 npm run test:aletheia:ui (6 passed: desktop/mobile matter-scoped AgentOps route, Aletheia local workspace, and Review Studio demo)
risks: Sensitive material flags are deterministic keyword flags, not a legal privilege determination.
Supabase document upload/search remains fail-closed; parity applies when existing source chunks are converted to evidence.
Evidence Registry stable anchors are in-page DOM anchors, not first-class artifact detail routes.
Persistence-level document sensitivity fields remain a later contract decision if deterministic keyword metadata is insufficient for gates.
